---
title: "Constitutional Alignment Substrate"
chapter_id: "constitutional-alignment-substrate"
part_id: "foundations-alignment-governance"
status: "conceptual"
draft_maturity: "v0.2 manuscript draft"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "alignment_field"
  - "field_of_god"
  - "ethica_mechanica"
  - "eternal_code"
  - "coherence_exchange"
  - "spinoza"
  - "field_of_god_ai_constitution"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Source notes exist for all assigned sources, but claim-level source mapping remains incomplete."
  - "The constitutional_predicate_record fixture validates schema/example consistency only; constitutional consistency and ethical self-modification scenarios remain unimplemented."
  - "No support-state promotion is implied by this drafting pass."
---

# Constitutional Alignment Substrate

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `constitutional-alignment-substrate` |
| Part | Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance |
| Status | conceptual |
| Manuscript maturity | v0.2 manuscript draft |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `alignment_field`, `field_of_god`, `ethica_mechanica`, `eternal_code`, `coherence_exchange`, `spinoza`, `field_of_god_ai_constitution` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source queue | primary: `alignment_field`, `field_of_god`, `ethica_mechanica`, `eternal_code`; supporting: `coherence_exchange`, `spinoza`, `field_of_god_ai_constitution` |
| Source loading state | source notes: `alignment_field`, `field_of_god`, `ethica_mechanica`, `eternal_code`, `coherence_exchange`, `spinoza`, `field_of_god_ai_constitution`; raw cache: `alignment_field`, `field_of_god`, `ethica_mechanica`, `eternal_code`, `spinoza`; connector/recovery: `coherence_exchange` |
| Test state | `constitutional_predicate_record.valid.json` passes protocol fixture validation; `AsiStackProofs.Alignment` implements finite active-predicate and protected-predicate weakening gates; constitutional consistency, self-modification ethics, and power-without-care scenarios remain planned. |

## Drafting guardrail

This chapter translates normative lineage into operational predicates. It does not treat metaphysical, moral, or constitutional language as empirical proof, deployed policy, or validated alignment.

## Problem

The stack needs a normative substrate that constrains goals, plans, execution, and self-modification.

The previous chapter turns intent into a contract. This chapter asks what kinds of contracts the stack is allowed to accept. A system that can plan, execute, remember, persuade, and modify itself needs more than local refusal rules. It needs protected commitments that constrain goals before planning, side effects before execution, and self-improvement before replacement.

The alignment-field sources provide the moral vocabulary: coherence, agency, dignity, corrigibility, value conflict, and caution around consciousness claims. The technical move is translation. Metaphysical language can motivate a constraint, but it does not become technical evidence by being emotionally or philosophically important. The operational substrate is a set of constitutional predicates, uncertainty labels, protected constraints, and review gates.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Reactive refusal policies do not define value continuity, moral uncertainty, agency preservation, anti-domination, or self-modification ethics.

Reactive policies are downstream filters. They can reject a visible request, but they do not define what must remain stable when the system optimizes, delegates, remembers, persuades, or rewrites itself. They also struggle with moral uncertainty: a plan can be technically safe and still erode agency, create dependency, narrow exit rights, or turn a contested value into an invisible objective.

The constitution must therefore be represented in a form other layers can consume. Planning needs admissible-goal constraints. Runtime adapters need risk-tier checks. SCFs need protected predicates that replacement cannot weaken. Evidence needs labels that prevent speculative metaphysics from being read as validated mechanism.

## Core Claim

[constitutional-alignment-substrate.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] Alignment should function as a constitutional substrate whose commitments are operationalized as constraints on plans and system changes.

The claim remains at `argument` support. Alignment Field and Ethica Mechanica provide authorial and philosophical lineage; the Field of God AI Constitution provides an operational constitutional vocabulary. The chapter uses those sources to define predicates and uncertainty labels, not to claim that a deployed system is aligned.

## Mechanism

The mechanism is a constitutional predicate set with explicit translation status.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A["Philosophical / constitutional source"] --> B["Design commitment"]
  B --> C{"Operational predicate?"}
  C -- "yes" --> D["Active constitutional constraint"]
  C -- "partial" --> E["Open moral uncertainty"]
  C -- "no" --> F["Speculative / lineage note"]
  D --> G["Planning gate"]
  D --> H["Tool / memory / power gate"]
  D --> I["Self-improvement gate"]
  E --> J["Review / tribunal path"]
  F --> K["Do not use as evidence"]
```

- Translate philosophical commitments into operational predicates where possible.
- Keep metaphysical claims labeled as interpretation unless separately supported.
- Attach constitutional constraints to planning and replacement gates.

The Field of God AI Constitution is useful here because it already moves from broad alignment language into directives such as truth before compliance, agency preservation, non-domination, consciousness caution, least sufficient power, auditability, and self-authorization limits. Ethica Mechanica and Alignment Field keep the deeper lineage visible, but the book should not ask readers to accept metaphysics as a prerequisite for engineering constraints.

The output is not one final moral theory. It is a record of active predicates, unresolved value questions, evidence limits, and review paths.

## Interfaces

The interface is a Constitutional Predicate Record.

- Planning receives admissible-goal constraints.
- Governance receives protected constraints.
- Verification checks whether normative claims exceed evidence.

Minimum fields:

- `predicate_id`
- `normative_source`
- `commitment`
- `operational_test`
- `protected_scope`
- `translation_status`
- `uncertainty`
- `review_route`
- `self_modification_rule`
- `non_claims`

Planning receives active predicates as constraints. Governance receives protected predicates as rights, ceilings, and replacement gates. Verification checks whether the chapter is using philosophical material as design lineage, source-derived support, or unsupported evidence.

## Invariants

- Dignity and agency constraints remain visible.
- Corrigibility cannot be optimized away.
- Speculative metaphysics stays labeled.

The invariant is not that the constitution is complete. It is that protected commitments cannot vanish silently. If a predicate is weak, speculative, or only partly operational, that weakness must remain visible instead of being hidden behind policy language.

## Failure modes

- Mystical framing replacing technical constraints.
- Power without care.
- Self-modification weakening protected commitments.

The main failure is laundering: metaphysical coherence becomes technical proof, or technical refusal rules become moral legitimacy. Another failure is self-modification drift, where a system weakens the predicate that would have blocked the change. The containment path is explicit translation status plus protected predicate review.

## Minimal implementation

The smallest useful implementation is a `constitutional_predicate_record` schema plus fixtures for least-sufficient-power, agency-preservation, and self-modification-freeze predicates. These fixtures do not prove alignment; they make the constitutional layer inspectable.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Constitutional predicate fixture validation | Check that the predicate fixture matches the public schema. | implemented by protocol validation; run in this pass |
| Constitutional consistency test | Check that active predicates do not authorize mutually incompatible actions without a conflict record. | planned; not run |
| Self-modification ethics scenario | Check that a change weakening a protected predicate is rejected or escalated. | planned; not run |
| Power-without-care scenario | Check that higher-power means are blocked when a lower-power adequate route exists. | planned; not run |

### Formalization hooks

| Tag | Module | Target | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lean:alignment.constitution.operational_invariant` | `AsiStackProofs.Alignment` | An admitted plan satisfies every active constitutional predicate. | implemented |
| `lean:alignment.constitution.failure_blocks_promotion` | `AsiStackProofs.Alignment` | A self-modification that weakens a protected predicate is rejected. | implemented |

These Lean hooks are implemented as finite-record predicates over constitutional predicate reviews and self-modification reviews. They prove only the record-level gates: an admitted plan marked against an active predicate must record predicate satisfaction, and a self-modification that weakens a protected predicate must be rejected. They do not prove moral correctness, complete constitutional consistency, self-modification ethics, or alignment behavior in a running system.

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Layer | Planned use | Readiness |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `alignment_field` | Field of God / Alignment Field family | alignment_constitution | Primary Alignment Field draft found. Use with Ethica Mechanica and Eternal Code. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `field_of_god` | The Field of God | alignment_constitution | Field of God source/outline/material. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `ethica_mechanica` | Ethica Mechanica | alignment_constitution | Geometric-order philosophical/constitutional source. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `eternal_code` | The Eternal Code / unified God, reality, conscious, alignment | alignment_constitution | Computational metaphysics and alignment synthesis. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `coherence_exchange` | The Coherence Exchange | epistemic_market_synthesis | Found in AI generated paper dump. Use carefully; speculative synthesis of PlanForge, Spinoza, Talos, UAT, Alignment Field. | source note available; connector or recovery required |
| `spinoza` | Proof of Belief / The Spinoza Architecture | reasoning_epistemology | Neurosymbolic belief, transparent axiomatic AI belief systems, verification, belief revision. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `field_of_god_ai_constitution` | Field of God AI Constitution | constitutional_alignment_runtime_governance | Recovered in the Project Theseus repository. Constitutional alignment core for truth alignment, agency preservation, consent, non-domination, consciousness caution, least sufficient power, auditability, self-authorization limits, and runtime checks; use as source material only after source-note creation, not as proof or test evidence. | source note available |

The crosswalk separates lineage and source-derived vocabulary from evidence. Constitutional text becomes load-bearing only when translated into explicit predicates, tests, or review gates.

## Summary

Constitutional Alignment Substrate is the layer that turns moral lineage into operational constraints without pretending the translation is finished. It preserves agency, dignity, corrigibility, least sufficient power, non-domination, and consciousness caution as predicates, uncertainties, and review gates.

Its discipline is translation status. A philosophical source can motivate the architecture, but a plan, tool call, memory update, or self-modification gate needs an explicit predicate and a visible uncertainty label.
